sections.paragraph:
διαβολή, ῆς, ἡ (s. διάβολος; Epicharmus, Hdt. et al.; PSI 441, 19 [III b.c.]; Sb 9558, 7; LXX; TestJos 1:7; EpArist; Jos., Ant. 6, 285 and 286, Vi. 80; Mel., HE 4, 26, 9; Ath.) slander (w. καταλαλιά) Pol 4:3. (For usage in magical pap, PUps 8, p. 115.)—DELG s.v. βάλλω p. 162. TW.

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

PUps 8 PUps 8 = PUpsaliensis 8—List 4

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 226
